worker_status.description: Operational status of a Worker declaration. active means the daemon loads and runs it. paused means the daemon skips it without retiring it. deprecated means it is retained for reference only and will not be loaded.

blackboard_entry_status.description: Canonical lifecycle states for all Blackboard entries. open and claimed are active (claimable or held). resolved, abandoned, deferred_to_proposal, dry_run_complete, and indeterminate are terminal — no worker may claim or transition them further without explicit revival.

blackboard_entry_status_active.description: The subset of blackboard_entry_status values that are non-terminal. A Worker claiming findings MUST filter to these values only.

proposal_status.description: Canonical lifecycle states for Proposals. draft, pending, approved, and executing are active — deduplication must treat these as blocking a re-creation of the same action group. completed and failed are terminal.

proposal_status_active.description: The subset of proposal_status values that are active. RemediatorWorker MUST check for proposals in these states before creating a new one for the same action group.

proposal_risk.description: Risk classification for a Proposal. Determines whether approval_required is true. safe proposals are auto-approved. moderate and high require human review.

proposal_approval_authority.description: Closed set of authority values for the approval_authority column on core.autonomous_proposals. Identifies the rule or role under which a proposal was approved. Non-omittable when status transitions to approved, executing, or completed (per URS NFR.5; URS Q2.A; ADR-015 D6). risk_classification.safe_auto_approval is the autonomous self-promote authority, used when compute_risk() classifies the proposal as safe and approval_required is False. human.cli_operator is the authority for approvals issued via the core-admin proposals approve CLI.

dry_run_scope.description: Disambiguates the three distinct dry-run modes in CORE. evaluation_only means the rule was checked but no fix was generated. fix_generated means a fix was produced but not applied. finding_suppressed means the finding is tagged for exclusion from remediation pipelines.
